segment_definition: Identify all speech segments per speaker with consistent IDs, assigning them in the order they first appear (spk00, spk01, etc.). Keep non-adjacent turns separate even for the same speaker. For simultaneous speech, use a comma-separated speaker_id (e.g., spk00,spk01) in a single segment. Segments must not overlap in time.
metadata_schema: {"name": "speaker_segment", "strict": true, "schema": {"type": "object", "properties": {"start_time": {"type": "number", "description": "Start time of the segment in seconds."}, "end_time": {"type": "number", "description": "End time of the segment in seconds."}, "speaker_id": {"type": "string", "description": "Unique speaker labels assigned in the order of their first appearance (spk00, spk01, etc.). Use comma-separated values (e.g., spk00,spk01) for simultaneous speech."}}, "required": ["start_time", "end_time", "speaker_id"], "additionalProperties": false}}

segment_definition: Find all segments where the spoken language is French. Merge consecutive segments into a single segment if they are temporally adjacent and have the same language, and only if there is no other language present between the end of one segment and the start of the next. Even if other languages are also heard in the segment, include all segments where French is audible.
metadata_schema: {"schema": {"type": "object", "properties": {"start_time": {"type": "number", "description": "Start timestamp in seconds."}, "end_time": {"type": "number", "description": "End timestamp in seconds."}}, "required": ["start_time", "end_time"], "additionalProperties": false}, "name": "a8_metadata", "strict": true}

metadata_schema: {"name": "segment_wise_metadata_extraction", "strict": true, "schema": {"type": "object", "properties": {"start_time": {"type": "number", "description": "Start timestamp in seconds from the beginning of the video."}, "end_time": {"type": "number", "description": "End timestamp in seconds from the beginning of the video."}, "topic": {"type": "string", "description": "The primary subject matter or topic of this segment (e.g., Politics, Sports, Nature)."}, "summary": {"type": "string", "description": "A concise textual overview focusing on the narrative arc, main actions, and plot progression (Scene Summary)."}}, "required": ["start_time", "end_time", "topic", "summary"], "additionalProperties": false}}

segment_definition: Find every jump ball in this video. Segment starts when the referee holds the ball between two opposing players at a tip-off circle. Segment ends when the referee tosses the ball and one player tips it, establishing the direction of possession. Include both game/overtime tipoffs and held-ball jump balls.

segment_definition: Find every free throw sequence in this video. The segment starts when the referee hands the ball to the shooter at the free throw line. The segment ends when the final free throw attempt in the sequence is resolved — the ball either passes through the net (made) or bounces off the rim or backboard (missed). All attempts within a single awarded sequence are captured as one segment.

segment_definition: Find every three-point attempt in this video. A segment starts when the shooter's feet are clearly positioned behind the three-point arc at the moment they initiate upward shot motion — the first visible upward movement into jump or set-shot form with both feet behind the arc. A segment ends at the first of: the ball passing through the net (made), the ball making first contact with the rim or backboard (missed), or a defender's hand contacting and deflecting the ball before it reaches the rim (blocked — end point is the instant of hand-to-ball contact). Attempts where the shooter's foot is on or inside the arc at the moment shot motion begins should not be included.

segment_definition: Find every free throw sequence in this video. The segment starts at the moment the foul is clearly visible on screen — illegal contact between players is observable — or when the referee's whistle sounds to stop play (for technical fouls where contact may not be visually apparent). The segment ends after the final free throw attempt is resolved and all players have returned to their court positions with the next play imminent (inbound pass or jump ball ready to begin). This extended window captures the full play stoppage: the foul moment, player and coach reactions, free throw line setup, all attempts, and post-sequence court positioning.

segment_definition: Find every possession in this video. A segment starts at the moment a team first gains clear, controlled possession of the ball after a change: the instant a rebound is secured (ball clearly under a single player's grip), the moment a steal puts the ball in the defender's hands, the receiving player's first controlled touch on an inbound pass, or the moment the ball passes through the net on the opponent's made basket (which triggers an inbound). A segment ends at the first of: the ball leaving the shooter's hands on a field goal attempt, a turnover visibly committed (bad pass leaves the passer's hands, a travel step occurs, or the ball is stripped from the ball-handler), a referee's whistle indicating a foul, or the period clock reaching zero. Each possession from both teams is an independent segment.
metadata_schema: {"name": "H16", "strict": false, "schema": {"properties": {"start_time": {"type": "number", "description": "Timestamp (seconds) of segment start \u2014 first controlled touch after change of possession"}, "end_time": {"type": "number", "description": "Timestamp (seconds) of segment end \u2014 shot released, turnover committed, foul whistle, or period expiration"}, "quarter_number": {"type": "integer", "enum": [1, 2, 3, 4], "description": "Quarter of the game"}, "period_type": {"type": "string", "enum": ["regulation", "overtime"], "description": "Whether segment occurs in regulation or OT"}, "possession_team": {"type": "string", "description": "Team on offense during this possession"}, "possession_result": {"type": "string", "enum": ["made_fg", "missed_fg", "turnover", "foul_drawn", "end_of_period"], "description": "How the possession ultimately ended. Reflects the final outcome after any offensive rebound extensions."}, "had_offensive_rebound": {"type": "boolean", "description": "True if the possession was extended by at least one offensive rebound before concluding"}, "points_scored": {"type": "integer", "description": "Total points scored on this possession (0 if no score; includes free throws from an and-one)"}, "play_type": {"type": "string", "enum": ["iso", "pick_and_roll", "transition", "post_up", "spot_up", "set_play", "other"], "description": "Dominant play type that best characterizes this possession"}, "change_of_possession_trigger": {"type": "string", "enum": ["defensive_rebound", "offensive_rebound", "steal", "inbound_after_made_basket", "inbound_after_dead_ball_turnover", "jump_ball_won"], "description": "The specific event that ended the previous possession and started this one"}, "shot_clock_at_start": {"type": "number", "description": "Shot clock remaining (seconds) at the moment possession begins"}, "is_replay": {"type": "boolean", "description": "True if the segment is broadcast replay footage rather than live action"}}, "required": ["start_time", "end_time", "quarter_number", "period_type", "possession_team", "possession_result", "had_offensive_rebound", "points_scored", "play_type", "change_of_possession_trigger", "shot_clock_at_start", "is_replay"], "additionalProperties": false}}

segment_definition: Find every timeout in this video. Segment starts when the referee blows the whistle and signals timeout — making a T-signal with both hands or pointing to the bench. Segment ends when the referee blows the whistle to resume play and both teams are back in position with an inbound pass or jump ball imminent.

segment_definition: Find every and-one scoring play in this video. The segment starts when the shooter begins the upward shot motion on a live-action field goal attempt. The segment ends when the referee visibly signals both that the basket counted and that a foul was called on the play — the end boundary is the completion of the referee's foul confirmation gesture, not the moment the ball passes through the net. The ball passing through the net is an intermediate event within the segment, not the boundary. The subsequent bonus free throw attempt is a separate unit and is not included in this segment.

the bonus free throw is a separate unit"}, "shot_type": {"type": "string", "enum": ["layup", "dunk", "mid_range", "three_pointer", "floater", "hook_shot", "tip_in", "alley_oop", "putback", "other"], "description": "Type of shot taken"}, "shot_zone": {"type": "string", "enum": ["paint", "mid_range", "corner_three", "wing_three", "top_of_key", "other"], "description": "Court zone from which the shot was taken"}, "foul_type": {"type": "string", "enum": ["shooting"], "description": "Type of foul \u2014 always 'shooting' for an and-one play"}, "free_throws_awarded": {"type": "integer", "description": "Number of bonus free throws awarded \u2014 always 1 for an and-one"}, "score_before": {"type": "object", "properties": {"home": {"type": "integer"}, "away": {"type": "integer"}}, "description": "Scoreline immediately before the shot"}, "score_after_basket": {"type": "object", "properties": {"home": {"type": "integer"}, "away": {"type": "integer"}}, "description": "Scoreline after the made basket and before the bonus free throw attempt"}, "signal_delay_seconds": {"type": "number", "description": "Time in seconds between the ball passing through the net and the referee completing the foul confirmation signal"}, "is_replay": {"type": "boolean", "description": "True if the segment is broadcast replay footage, not live action"}}, "required": ["start_time", "end_time", "quarter_number", "period_type", "points_type", "shot_type", "shot_zone", "foul_type", "free_throws_awarded", "score_before", "score_after_basket", "signal_delay_seconds", "is_replay"], "additionalProperties": false}}

segment_definition: Find every timeout in this video. Segment starts when the first visible timeout request gesture is made — a player raises a T-signal with both hands, or a coach on the sideline visibly waves repeatedly or signals toward a referee. Segment ends when the referee acknowledges the timeout by blowing the whistle and making the official T-signal. Capture only the request-to-acknowledgment window; the full timeout stoppage that follows is a separate unit.

segment_definition: Find every possession that ends in a turnover while the offensive team is leading. A turnover possession is one where the offensive team loses the ball without completing a field goal attempt or drawing a foul — including bad passes, steals, travels, shot clock violations, and other violations. 'While leading' means the possessing team's score is strictly greater than their opponent's score at the moment the turnover occurs. Segment boundary starts when the offensive team gains first controlled possession following any change of possession — first controlled touch after an inbound pass, rebound secured, steal, or opponent's made basket — and ends at the moment the turnover is committed: the instant a bad pass leaves the passer's hands, a travel step occurs, or the ball is stripped from the ball-handler's control.
metadata_schema: {"name": "H16", "strict": false, "schema": {"properties": {"start_time": {"type": "number", "description": "Timestamp (seconds) of segment start \u2014 moment the offensive team gains first controlled possession"}, "end_time": {"type": "number", "description": "Timestamp (seconds) of segment end \u2014 moment turnover is committed (bad pass leaves hands, travel step occurs, or ball stripped from ball-handler)"}, "quarter_number": {"type": "integer", "enum": [1, 2, 3, 4], "description": "Quarter of the game"}, "period_type": {"type": "string", "enum": ["regulation", "overtime"], "description": "Whether segment is in regulation or OT"}, "possession_team": {"type": "string", "description": "Team on offense during the possession (the team that commits the turnover)"}, "possession_result": {"type": "string", "enum": ["turnover"], "description": "Always 'turnover' for this query \u2014 the possession ended with the offense losing the ball to the defense"}, "turnover_type": {"type": "string", "enum": ["stolen", "out_of_bounds", "travel", "shot_clock_violation", "offensive_foul", "bad_pass", "other"], "description": "Specific mechanism by which the turnover occurred"}, "is_live_ball": {"type": "boolean", "description": "True if the ball remains in play immediately after the turnover (e.g. steal, deflection still in bounds \u2014 defense can immediately transition). False for dead-ball turnovers (out of bounds, offensive foul, violation) where play stops and resumes with an inbound."}, "leads_to_fast_break": {"type": "boolean", "description": "True if the opposing team converts this turnover into a fast break"}, "play_type": {"type": "string", "enum": ["iso", "pick_and_roll", "transition", "post_up", "spot_up", "set_play", "other"], "description": "The dominant tactical pattern of the possession that ended in the turnover"}, "had_offensive_rebound": {"type": "boolean", "description": "True if the possession was extended by at least one offensive rebound before the turnover ultimately occurred"}, "score_differential": {"type": "integer", "description": "Signed point differential at the moment the turnover is committed (possession_team_score \u2212 opponent_score). segment_definition: Find every three-point attempt taken from the wing area that results in a miss. A wing three-point attempt is defined as a shot where the shooter's feet are clearly behind the three-point arc at the moment of release, taken from the left or right wing — the arc region between the corner and the top of the key. A missed attempt is one where the ball contacts the rim or backboard and does not pass through the net, excluding blocked shots. Segment boundary starts when the shooter's feet are clearly behind the three-point arc and upward shot motion is initiated, and ends when the ball bounces off the rim or backboard.
metadata_schema: {"name": "H16", "strict": false, "schema": {"properties": {"start_time": {"type": "number", "description": "Timestamp (seconds) of segment start \u2014 when upward shot motion begins with shooter's feet behind the arc on the wing"}, "end_time": {"type": "number", "description": "Timestamp (seconds) of segment end \u2014 when the ball bounces off the rim or backboard"}, "quarter_number": {"type": "integer", "enum": [1, 2, 3, 4], "description": "Quarter in which the attempt occurred"}, "period_type": {"type": "string", "enum": ["regulation", "overtime"], "description": "Whether the attempt occurred in regulation or overtime"}, "shot_zone": {"type": "string", "enum": ["left_wing_three", "right_wing_three"], "description": "Which wing the shot was taken from, from the offensive team's perspective"}, "shot_type": {"type": "string", "enum": ["catch_and_shoot", "step_back", "pull_up", "off_screen", "other"], "description": "The shot creation mechanic \u2014 how the shooter generated the attempt"}, "shot_result": {"type": "string", "enum": ["missed"], "description": "Always 'missed' for this query \u2014 ball contacts rim or backboard without passing through the net"}, "contest_level": {"type": "string", "enum": ["open", "contested", "heavily_contested"], "description": "Degree of defensive pressure at moment of release. Positive = shooting team leading; negative = shooting team trailing; 0 = tied."}, "clock_remaining": {"type": "string", "description": "Game clock at the moment of shot release, e.g. 'Q1 10:42'"}, "is_replay": {"type": "boolean", "description": "True if the segment is broadcast replay footage rather than live action"}}, "required": ["start_time", "end_time", "quarter_number", "period_type", "shot_zone", "shot_type", "shot_result", "contest_level", "shot_distance", "rebound_outcome", "score_differential", "clock_remaining", "is_replay"], "additionalProperties": false}}

segment_definition: Find every field goal attempt in the paint that is blocked. A paint attempt is any shot taken from within the key/lane area — the painted rectangular zone extending from the baseline to the free throw line, approximately 15 feet from the basket and 16 feet wide, including the restricted area under the basket. A blocked attempt ends at the instant the defender's hand contacts the ball and deflects its trajectory, before the ball reaches or passes through the rim. The segment starts when the shooter clearly initiates upward shot motion and ends at the instant of hand-to-ball contact by the defender.

'help_side' = rotated from weakside; 'on_ball' = primary defender contests directly; 'from_behind' = trailing defender reaches over; 'chase_down' = fast-break or transition recovery block."}, "score_differential": {"type": "integer", "description": "Signed point differential at the moment of the attempt (shooting team score \u2212 opponent score). Positive = shooting team leading; negative = shooting team trailing; 0 = tied."}, "score_before": {"type": "object", "properties": {"home": {"type": "integer"}, "away": {"type": "integer"}}, "description": "Scoreboard immediately before the shot attempt, e.g. {\"home\": 3, \"away\": 0}"}, "is_replay": {"type": "boolean", "description": "True if the segment is broadcast replay footage rather than live action"}}, "required": ["start_time", "end_time", "quarter_number", "period_type", "shot_type", "shot_result", "shot_zone", "shot_distance", "block_height", "defender_body_position", "score_differential", "score_before", "is_replay"], "additionalProperties": false}}

segment_definition: Find every three-point attempt that results in a made basket while the shooting team is trailing. 'Trailing' means the shooting team has fewer points than the opponent at the moment of shot release (score_differential < 0). Segment boundary starts when the shooter's feet are clearly behind the three-point arc at the moment upward shot motion begins, and ends when the ball passes through the net.
metadata_schema: {"name": "H16", "strict": false, "schema": {"properties": {"start_time": {"type": "number", "description": "Timestamp (seconds) of segment start \u2014 shooter's upward motion initiates with feet behind the arc"}, "end_time": {"type": "number", "description": "Timestamp (seconds) of segment end \u2014 ball passes through the net"}, "quarter_number": {"type": "integer", "enum": [1, 2, 3, 4], "description": "Quarter in which the made three-pointer occurred"}, "period_type": {"type": "string", "enum": ["regulation", "overtime"], "description": "Whether the shot occurred in regulation or overtime"}, "shot_zone": {"type": "string", "enum": ["corner_three", "wing_three", "top_of_key"], "description": "Area behind the arc from which the shot was taken"}, "shot_distance": {"type": "integer", "description": "Approximate shot distance in feet from the basket at moment of release"}, "shot_result": {"type": "string", "enum": ["made"], "description": "Always 'made' \u2014 this query filters to successful three-pointers only"}, "is_and_one": {"type": "boolean", "description": "True if the made three-pointer was accompanied by a shooting foul, adding one free throw"}, "is_assisted": {"type": "boolean", "description": "True if the basket was preceded by a teammate's pass directly enabling the shot (assist credited)"}, "shot_creation_type": {"type": "string", "enum": ["catch_and_shoot", "off_dribble", "step_back", "transition_pull_up", "other"], "description": "How the shooter created the shot opportunity \u2014 'catch_and_shoot' = shooter was stationary or relocating to catch a pass with no dribble; 'off_dribble' = shooter pulled up off live dribble; 'step_back' = shooter created separation with a step-back move; 'transition_pull_up' = shot taken in open court before defense set"}, "score_differential": {"type": "integer", "description": "Signed point differential at moment of release: shooting_team_score \u2212 opponent_score. segment_definition: Find every fast break that results in a made field goal while the game is within one possession at the moment the transition begins. A one-possession game is defined as a score differential of 3 points or fewer (|score_differential| ≤ 3) at the instant the fast break is triggered. Segment boundary starts from the triggering event that initiated the transition — a steal secured, defensive rebound controlled, blocked shot recovered, or the opponent's made basket triggering the inbound — and ends when the ball passes through the net on the made field goal.
metadata_schema: {"name": "H16", "strict": false, "schema": {"properties": {"start_time": {"type": "number", "description": "Timestamp (seconds) of segment start \u2014 the instant of the triggering event (steal, rebound secured, blocked shot recovered, or opponent's made basket)"}, "end_time": {"type": "number", "description": "Timestamp (seconds) of segment end \u2014 ball passes through the net on the made field goal"}, "quarter_number": {"type": "integer", "enum": [1, 2, 3, 4], "description": "Quarter in which the fast break occurs"}, "period_type": {"type": "string", "enum": ["regulation", "overtime"], "description": "Whether the fast break occurs in regulation or OT"}, "transition_origin": {"type": "string", "enum": ["turnover", "defensive_rebound", "blocked_shot_recovered", "made_basket"], "description": "The triggering event that initiated the fast break and defines the segment start boundary"}, "fast_break_result": {"type": "string", "enum": ["made_fg"], "description": "Outcome of the fast break \u2014 always made_fg for segments returned by this query"}, "shot_type": {"type": "string", "enum": ["layup", "dunk", "three_pointer", "floater", "alley_oop", "tip_in", "other"], "description": "Type of shot used to finish the fast break"}, "shot_zone": {"type": "string", "enum": ["paint", "transition_lane", "three_point", "corner_three", "wing_three", "top_of_key", "other"], "description": "Zone from which the finishing shot was taken"}, "players_involved": {"type": "integer", "description": "Number of offensive players actively participating in the fast break"}, "break_lane": {"type": "string", "enum": ["left", "center", "right"], "description": "Primary lane the fast break developed along from the offensive team's perspective"}, "score_differential": {"type": "integer", "description": "Signed point differential at the moment the fast break is triggered (reference_team_score \u2212 opponent_score, where reference team = the team executing the fast break). Confirms |score_differential| \u2264 3."}, "score_before": {"type": "object", "properties": {"home": {"type": "integer"}, "away": {"type": "integer"}}, "description": "Score at the moment the fast break is triggered (segment start)"}, "score_after": {"type": "object", "properties": {"home": {"type": "integer"}, "away": {"type": "integer"}}, "description": "Score immediately after the made field goal"}, "points_scored": {"type": "integer", "enum": [2, 3], "description": "Points scored on the fast break finish (2 for a made two-pointer, 3 for a made three-pointer)"}, "lead_changed": {"type": "boolean", "description": "True if the made field goal on this fast break changed which team was leading (or broke a tie to create a lead)"}, "is_replay": {"type": "boolean", "description": "True if the segment is broadcast replay footage rather than live action"}}, "required": ["start_time", "end_time", "quarter_number", "period_type", "transition_origin", "fast_break_result", "shot_type", "shot_zone", "players_involved", "break_lane", "score_differential", "score_before", "score_after", "points_scored", "lead_changed", "is_replay"], "additionalProperties": false}}

segment_definition: Find every three-point attempt that results in a made basket while the shooting team is trailing. 'While trailing' means the shooting team has fewer points than the opponent at the exact moment upward shot motion begins. Segment boundary starts when the shooter's feet are clearly behind the three-point arc and upward shot motion begins, and ends when the ball passes through the net.

segment_definition: Find every scoring play that results in an and-one while the game is a one-possession game. An and-one is a made live-action field goal on which the shooter simultaneously draws a shooting foul, earning one additional free throw. A one-possession game is defined as a score differential of 3 points or fewer (|reference_team_score − opponent_score| ≤ 3) at the moment the shot is released. Segment boundary starts when the shooter begins upward shot motion on a live-action field goal, and ends when the referee visibly signals both that the basket is counted and that a foul has been called.

Must satisfy |score_differential| \u2264 3 to qualify as a one-possession game. segment_definition: Find every three-point attempt that results in a successful make while the shooting team holds a two-possession lead at the moment of release. A two-possession lead is defined as a score differential of 4 to 6 points in favor of the shooting team (shooting_team_score − opponent_score = 4, 5, or 6). Segment boundary starts when the shooter's feet are clearly behind the three-point arc at the moment upward shot motion begins and ends when the ball passes through the net.

segment_definition: Find every scoring play in the paint while the scoring team is leading. 'Paint' means the shot is released from within the key/lane area — the painted rectangular zone extending from the baseline to the free throw line (~15 feet from the basket, 16 feet wide), including the restricted area directly under the basket. 'While leading' means the scoring team holds more points than the opponent at the moment of the shot (score differential > 0, where score differential = scoring team score − opponent score). Segment boundary starts when the scorer begins upward shot motion and ends when the ball passes through the net.

segment_definition: Find every fast break that results in a made field goal while the scoring team is trailing. A fast break begins when a team gains the ball in transition — via steal secured, defensive rebound controlled, blocked shot recovered, or opponent's made basket triggering an inbound — and ends when a shot is released, a foul is drawn, or a turnover is committed. 'Trailing' means the team executing the fast break had fewer points than their opponent at the moment the break was initiated (score_differential < 0). Segment boundary starts at the triggering event that initiates the transition (the steal, defensive rebound secured, blocked shot recovered, or opponent's made basket), and ends the instant the ball passes through the net on the made basket.

Must be negative (team is trailing). Reference team = the team executing the fast break."}, "break_lane": {"type": "string", "enum": ["left", "center", "right"], "description": "Primary lane the fast break developed along from the offensive team's perspective \u2014 'center' = straight down the middle, 'left'/'right' = ball attacks down the respective wing"}, "players_involved": {"type": "integer", "description": "Number of offensive players actively involved in the fast break (ball handler plus any teammates in the break lane who were legitimate passing options)"}, "shot_type": {"type": "string", "enum": ["layup", "dunk", "floater", "alley_oop", "tip_in", "mid_range", "three_pointer", "other"], "description": "Type of field goal attempt that finished the fast break"}, "shot_zone": {"type": "string", "enum": ["paint", "mid_range", "three_point", "corner_three", "wing_three", "top_of_key", "other"], "description": "Floor zone from which the finishing shot was taken"}, "points_scored": {"type": "integer", "enum": [2, 3], "description": "Points scored on the made fast-break basket"}, "is_and_one": {"type": "boolean", "description": "True if the made basket was accompanied by a shooting foul, earning an additional free throw"}, "score_before": {"type": "object", "properties": {"home": {"type": "integer"}, "away": {"type": "integer"}}, "description": "Scoreboard at the moment the fast break is triggered, e.g. {\"home\": 26, \"away\": 10}"}, "score_after": {"type": "object", "properties": {"home": {"type": "integer"}, "away": {"type": "integer"}}, "description": "Scoreboard immediately after the made basket resolves, e.g. {\"home\": 26, \"away\": 12}"}, "clock_remaining": {"type": "string", "description": "Game clock at the moment the fast break is triggered, e.g. 'Q1 03:35'"}, "additionalProperties": false}}

segment_definition: Find every field_goal attempt that was blocked during the final two minutes of a half. A blocked field goal is defined as a defensive player deflecting the kick before it travels toward the uprights, causing it to fall short or to the ground. The final two minutes is defined as any time the game clock shows 2:00 or less remaining in the second or fourth quarter. The segment boundary starts at the snap — the moment the ball first moves backward from the center — and ends when the kick result is visually confirmed: either the ball is deflected and hits the ground, or the referee signals the result.

segment_definition: Find every turnover_play that begins in a goal-to-go situation. A goal-to-go situation is defined as a 1st down with the line of scrimmage at or inside the opponent's 10-yard line. A turnover_play is any scrimmage play that ends with the defense gaining possession via fumble recovery or interception. Segment boundary starts when the ball first moves backward from center (snap) and ends when the ball is visibly secured by the new possession team.

segment_definition: Find every play_scrimmage on 3rd down while the offense is trailing. Third down is the third attempt at gaining a first down in a series of downs. Trailing means the offensive team's score is strictly less than the defensive team's score at the moment of snap. Segment starts when the ball first moves backward from center (the snap) and ends when the ball is declared dead — defined as the ball carrier visibly motionless on the ground, ball going out of bounds, an incomplete pass hitting the turf, or a score.

segment_definition: Find every scrimmage play on 3rd down with 7 or more yards to go while the offense holds a two-score lead. Third-and-long is defined as 3rd down with at least 7 yards needed for a first down. A two-score lead is defined as a point differential of 9 to 16 points in favor of the offense at the time of the snap. Segment boundary starts when the offense breaks the huddle and sets into formation at the line of scrimmage (pre-snap alignment visible) and ends when the ball is declared dead.

segment_definition: Find every play_scrimmage that occurs under two minutes remaining in the fourth quarter while the offense is trailing by a two-score deficit. 'Under two minutes remaining' is defined as the game clock reading 2:00 or less in the fourth quarter. A 'two-score deficit' is defined as the offensive team trailing by 9–16 points at the time of the snap, meaning two possessions are required to tie or take the lead. Segment boundary starts at the snap — the moment the ball first moves backward from center — and ends at the dead ball, when play is visibly stopped: the carrier is motionless, the ball goes out of bounds, an incompletion is confirmed, or a score is achieved.

segment_definition: Find every scrimmage play in this video. Segment starts at the moment the first defensive player makes visible physical contact with the ball carrier — observable as body collision, arms wrapping, or tackle initiation — and ends when play action has visibly stopped: carrier motionless with defenders on top, ball out of bounds, incomplete pass touching the ground, or score confirmed. For plays where no defender contact occurs before the dead ball (e.g. uncontested scores or clear-away catches), use the dead ball moment as both the start and the contact description.

segment_definition: Find every play from scrimmage that originates in the offense's own territory while the score differential is within one score. 'Own territory' is defined as the ball spotted anywhere between the offense's own goal line and their own 50-yard line, inclusive. A 'one-score game' is defined as a score differential of 8 or fewer points at the time of the snap — meaning a single possession, including a possible two-point conversion, could tie the game or take the lead. Segment starts when the ball visibly moves backward from center at the snap and ends when the play is declared dead: the ball carrier is motionless or stopped, the ball goes out of bounds, or an incomplete pass strikes the ground.

magnitude must be \u2264 8"}, "play_context": {"oneOf": [{"type": "array", "items": {"type": "string"}}, {"type": "null"}], "description": "Situational tags at snap, e.g. ['shotgun', 'no_huddle', 'hurry_up', 'two_tight_ends']"}, "is_replay": {"type": "boolean", "description": "True if the segment is broadcast replay footage, not live action"}}, "required": ["start_time", "end_time", "quarter_number", "half_number", "clock_at_snap", "down", "distance", "starting_field_position", "yards_from_own_goal", "play_type", "play_result", "yards_gained", "first_down_achieved", "score_at_snap", "score_differential", "play_context", "is_replay"], "additionalProperties": false}}

segment_definition: Find every set_of_downs that begins in the offense's own territory while the game is within one score. Own territory is defined as the offensive team's side of the field — their own 1- to 49-yard line. A one-score game is defined as a point differential of 8 or fewer points at the time of the first snap, meaning a single possession (touchdown plus two-point conversion) would tie or take the lead. Segment boundary starts at the snap of the first play in the down sequence and ends when the set concludes — a first down is gained, a score occurs, a turnover happens, a punt is executed, or a turnover on downs results.

segment_definition: Find every penalty play that occurs while the offense is trailing in a one-score game. A one-score game is defined as a score differential of 8 points or fewer at the moment of the snap, meaning a single possession (including a possible two-point conversion) could tie or win the game. 'Trailing' means the offensive team's score is strictly less than the defensive team's score at the snap. Segment boundary starts at the snap — when the ball first moves backward from center — and ends when the penalty flag is thrown and the play result is settled (ball declared dead and officiating stoppage complete).

segment_definition: Find every shot on target that is saved by the goalkeeper while the match is level, where the attempt is taken from outside the penalty area during the first half. Segment starts when the shooter's foot makes contact with the ball. Segment ends when the goalkeeper secures the ball and possession is clearly settled.
metadata_schema: {"name": "H16", "strict": false, "schema": {"properties": {"start_time": {"type": "number", "description": "Segment start (seconds)"}, "end_time": {"type": "number", "description": "Segment end (seconds)"}, "match_period": {"type": "string", "enum": ["first_half", "second_half", "extra_time_1", "extra_time_2", "penalty_shoot_out"], "description": "Game period in which the event occurs"}, "shooter": {"type": "string", "description": "Player who shot"}, "team": {"type": "string", "description": "Attacking team"}, "shot_result": {"type": "string", "enum": ["saved", "scored"], "description": "Outcome of the shot"}, "location": {"type": "string", "enum": ["penalty_area", "six_yard_box", "outside_box", "central_channel", "wide_channel"], "description": "Area of the pitch from which the shot was taken"}, "display_time": {"type": "string", "description": "Broadcast clock at the moment the shot is taken"}}, "required": ["start_time", "end_time", "match_period", "shooter", "team", "shot_result", "location", "display_time"], "additionalProperties": false}}

segment_definition: Find every corner kick delivered into the penalty area that is claimed cleanly by the goalkeeper during the first half when the score is level. Segment starts when the ball crosses the goal line or touchline to award the corner. Segment ends when the goalkeeper secures the ball and play resumes.

segment_definition: Find every goal scored by Arsenal. The segment starts at the last pass or run directly leading to the goal attempt. The segment ends when play restarts for kickoff, including any goal replays shown before the restart.
metadata_schema: {"name": "H16", "strict": false, "schema": {"properties": {"start_time": {"type": "number", "description": "Segment start (seconds)"}, "end_time": {"type": "number", "description": "Segment end (seconds)"}, "match_period": {"type": "string", "enum": ["first_half", "second_half", "extra_time_1", "extra_time_2", "penalty_shoot_out"], "description": "Game period in which the event occurs"}, "scorer": {"type": "string", "description": "Player who scored"}, "team": {"type": "string", "description": "Scoring team"}, "score_before": {"type": "string", "description": "Score before goal, e.g. '1-0'"}, "score_after": {"type": "string", "description": "Score after goal, e.g. '2-0'"}, "assist": {"type": ["string", "null"], "description": "Player who provided the assist, null if not identifiable"}, "goal_type": {"type": "string", "enum": ["open_play", "corner_kick", "direct_free_kick", "indirect_free_kick", "penalty", "own_goal"], "description": "Situation from which the goal was scored \u2014 open_play: goal from open play, including headers from crosses; corner_kick: goal from corner delivery (direct olimpico or via header/deflection); direct_free_kick: ball goes directly into net from free kick; indirect_free_kick: goal after indirect free kick pass; penalty: penalty kick; own_goal: own goal regardless of situation"}, "display_time": {"type": "string", "description": "Broadcast clock when the ball crosses the goal line, e.g. '90+2'"}}, "required": ["start_time", "end_time", "match_period", "scorer", "team", "score_before", "score_after", "assist", "goal_type", "display_time"], "additionalProperties": false}}

segment_definition: Locate each free kick. Segment starts at the referee's whistle signalling the foul. Segment ends once the kick has been taken and the play's immediate outcome is established.

segment_definition: Find every stoppage time event involving Luke Shaw. Segment starts when play is stopped and medical staff enter the pitch to attend to Luke Shaw. Segment ends when Luke Shaw is removed from the pitch and play resumes.

segment_definition: Identify every counter attack sequence. Segment starts when a team secures the ball and players immediately advance toward the opponent's half in transition. Segment ends when a shot is taken, the ball crosses the goal line for a goal, the ball is dispossessed, or the ball exits the field.

segment_definition: Retrieve all header events from the broadcast. Segment starts at the moment of head-to-ball contact. Segment ends when the ball's immediate result becomes clear — a goal, a goalkeeper save, a defensive clearance, or the ball going out of play.

segment_definition:
Segment this news broadcast into editorial packages. For each segment, identify editorial package type.

Boundary rules:

Begin each segment at the first frame the new speaker appears or begins speaking.

When audio and visual cues conflict, use whichever boundary keeps the primary segment contextually complete.

Attach blank frames, fades, and dissolves to the preceding segment.

Split into separate segments when the story topic changes, even if the editorial package type remains the same.
metadata_schema: {"name": "editorial_package_metadata", "strict": true, "schema": {"type": "object", "properties": {"start_time": {"type": "number", "description": "Start time of the editorial segment in seconds."}, "end_time": {"type": "number", "description": "End time of the editorial segment in seconds."}, "editorial_package": {"type": "string", "enum": ["anchor_intro", "field_report", "interview", "graphics_package", "studio_wrap", "commercial_break"], "description": "Type of editorial package segment."}}, "required": ["start_time", "end_time", "editorial_package"], "additionalProperties": false}}

segment_definition:
Identify all shot transitions in this video clip. For each transition, provide the transition type and its boundaries.

Boundary rules:
- For hard_cut: segment_start is the last frame of the outgoing shot; 
  segment_end is the first frame of the incoming shot.
- For all other transition types: segment_start is the first frame where 
  the transition effect becomes visible; segment_end is the last frame 
  before the incoming scene is fully settled. A tolerance of 1–2 frames 
  is acceptable for non-hard-cut transitions.
metadata_schema: {"name": "l0_transition_metadata", "strict": true, "schema": {"type": "object", "properties": {"start_time": {"type": "number", "description": "Segment start (seconds)"}, "end_time": {"type": "number", "description": "Segment end (seconds)"}, "transition_type": {"type": "string", "description": "The type of shot transition. Select the closest matching value from the examples below. If none apply, describe the transition type directly using precise cinematographic terminology.", "examples": [{"value": "hard_cut", "description": "Instantaneous. No overlap or effect between frames."}, {"value": "fade_to", "description": "Outgoing scene dims to a solid color until fully black or white."}, {"value": "fade_from", "description": "Incoming scene emerges from a solid color (black or white)."}, {"value": "dissolve", "description": "Two scenes overlap simultaneously. Both visible at the same time. No focus loss."}, {"value": "wipe", "description": "Incoming scene displaces outgoing scene along a directional edge. No blending."}, {"value": "dip_to_black", "description": "Scene darkens to black then brightens into next scene. Single continuous event."}, {"value": "dip_to_white", "description": "Scene brightens to white then clears into next scene. Single continuous event."}, {"value": "graphic_transition", "description": "A branded or motion graphic covers the screen as the transition mechanism."}, {"value": "scroll_transition", "description": "Outgoing scene slides out directionally while incoming scene enters from the opposite side."}, {"value": "zoom_transition", "description": "Incoming scene grows or shrinks into frame. Scale change is the defining characteristic."}, {"value": "flash_transition", "description": "Intense burst of light dominates the frame at the transition point."}, {"value": "blur_transition", "description": "Outgoing scene loses optical focus, then incoming scene emerges back into focus. Blur is the intermediary."}]}}, "required": ["start_time", "end_time", "transition_type"], "additionalProperties": false}}
